prompts.abstract: A rectangular box with a slightly wider top and a flat base. The top surface is slightly tilted.
prompts.description: A rectangular box with a slightly wider top and a flat base. The top surface is slightly tilted.
prompts.beginner: The design involves creating a simple rectangular base. This base has a length of about 0.75 units, a width of 0.375 units, and a height of 0.375 units. It serves as a foundational element in larger designs.
prompts.intermediate: Create a rectangular base by starting with a new coordinate system and drawing a two-dimensional sketch of a rectangle on the first face. The sketch forms a closed loop with four lines, defining the outline of the rectangle. After drawing the sketch, scale it down to fit the desired size. The sketch is then transformed into a three-dimensional object by extruding it 0.375 units along the normal direction. This process creates a solid rectangular base with a length of approximately 0.75 units, a width of 0.375 units, and a height of 0.375 units. The final shape is a simple rectangular prism that can be used as a foundational component in more complex designs.
prompts.expert: Start by creating a new coordinate system with the Euler angles set to zero degrees in all axes and the translation vector also set to zero. Next, draw the first two-dimensional sketch on the first face. This sketch consists of a single loop made up of four lines. The first line starts at the origin (0.0, 0.0) and ends at (0.75, 0.0). The second line starts at (0.75, 0.0) and ends at (0.75, 0.375). The third line starts at (0.75, 0.375) and ends at (0.0, 0.375). Finally, the fourth line completes the loop by starting at (0.0, 0.375) and ending at the origin (0.0, 0.0). After drawing the sketch, apply a scaling factor of 0.75 to the entire sketch to adjust its size. Ensure that the sketch remains aligned with the coordinate system by rotating it using the Euler angles [0.0, 0.0, 0.0] and translating it using the translation vector [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]. Once the sketch is correctly positioned, extrude it 0.375 units along the normal direction to create a three-dimensional rectangular base. Do not extrude in the opposite direction. This extrusion will form a new solid body with the following dimensions: length of 0.7499935090853259, width of 0.37499675454266296, and height of 0.374996754542663.
prompts.all_level_data:
<level1>
Start by creating a new coordinate system with the Euler angles set to zero degrees in all axes and the translation vector also set to zero. Next, draw the first two-dimensional sketch on the first face. This sketch consists of a single loop made up of four lines. The first line starts at the origin (0.0, 0.0) and ends at (0.75, 0.0). The second line starts at (0.75, 0.0) and ends at (0.75, 0.375). The third line starts at (0.75, 0.375) and ends at (0.0, 0.375). Finally, the fourth line completes the loop by starting at (0.0, 0.375) and ending at the origin (0.0, 0.0). After drawing the sketch, apply a scaling factor of 0.75 to the entire sketch to adjust its size. Ensure that the sketch remains aligned with the coordinate system by rotating it using the Euler angles [0.0, 0.0, 0.0] and translating it using the translation vector [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]. Once the sketch is correctly positioned, extrude it 0.375 units along the normal direction to create a three-dimensional rectangular base. Do not extrude in the opposite direction. This extrusion will form a new solid body with the following dimensions: length of 0.7499935090853259, width of 0.37499675454266296, and height of 0.374996754542663.
</level1>

<level2>
Create a rectangular base by starting with a new coordinate system and drawing a two-dimensional sketch of a rectangle on the first face. The sketch forms a closed loop with four lines, defining the outline of the rectangle. After drawing the sketch, scale it down to fit the desired size. The sketch is then transformed into a three-dimensional object by extruding it 0.375 units along the normal direction. This process creates a solid rectangular base with a length of approximately 0.75 units, a width of 0.375 units, and a height of 0.375 units. The final shape is a simple rectangular prism that can be used as a foundational component in more complex designs.
</level2>

<level3>
The design involves creating a simple rectangular base. This base has a length of about 0.75 units, a width of 0.375 units, and a height of 0.375 units. It serves as a foundational element in larger designs.
</level3>
prompts.nli_data:
<part_1>
### Construct a Rectangular Base
#### Create a New Coordinate System
- Set the Euler angles to [0.0, 0.0, 0.0].
- Set the translation vector to [0.0, 0.0, 0.0].

#### Draw the 2D Sketch
- **Face 1:**
  - **Loop 1:**
    - **Line 1:** Start at (0.0, 0.0) and end at (0.75, 0.0).
    - **Line 2:** Start at (0.75, 0.0) and end at (0.75, 0.375).
    - **Line 3:** Start at (0.75, 0.375) and end at (0.0, 0.375).
    - **Line 4:** Start at (0.0, 0.375) and end at (0.0, 0.0).

#### Scale the 2D Sketch
- Apply a scaling factor of 0.75 to the 2D sketch.

#### Transform the 2D Sketch into 3D
- Rotate the sketch using the Euler angles [0.0, 0.0, 0.0].
- Translate the sketch using the translation vector [0.0, 0.0, 0.0].

#### Extrude the 2D Sketch
- Extrude the sketch 0.375 units towards the normal.
- Do not extrude in the opposite direction.
- Create a new solid body.

#### Dimensions
- Length: 0.7499935090853259
- Width: 0.37499675454266296
- Height: 0.374996754542663
</part_1>
